title: Fix app stuck on loading spinner — initialization deadlock

requirements.text: Audit and fix `frontend/src/App.tsx` so it never gets stuck in an indefinite loading/initializing state. The initialization flow must complete and render a visible UI within a bounded timeout regardless of backend query outcomes. Specifically: (1) add a hard timeout (e.g., 5 seconds) to the auth initialization check so that if `useInternetIdentity` does not resolve, the app falls back to showing the LandingPage rather than spinning forever; (2) ensure the backend profile-fetch query used to decide whether to show ProfileSetupModal or Dashboard does not block render — use React Query's `isLoading` vs `isFetching` states carefully and treat a query error as 'not registered' rather than a hanging state; (3) ensure `showLanding` is set to `true` whenever the initialization phase ends without a confirmed authenticated identity, so the LandingPage is always shown as the fallback.

quotes: Now it doesnt even open the web app stuck on loading

acceptanceCriteria: Opening the app always renders a visible UI (LandingPage, Dashboard, or ProfileSetupModal) within 5 seconds, never staying on a blank loading spinner indefinitely.
If the auth client fails to initialize or times out, the LandingPage is shown to the user.
If the backend profile query errors or times out, the app treats the user as unregistered and shows ProfileSetupModal (if authenticated) or LandingPage (if not).
Authenticated users who are already registered are still correctly routed to the Dashboard.
No regression: the post-auth navigation loop (landing page redirect after sign-in) must remain fixed.

requirements.text: Audit `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` and any query hooks called during app initialization to ensure they have explicit `retry: 0` or low retry counts and defined `staleTime`/`gcTime` so a failed or slow backend call does not cause React Query to silently retry and block the UI indefinitely on first load.

quotes: likely a runtime initialization issue in App.tsx, possibly caused by the auth state check or a backend query hanging/erroring on load and blocking the render

acceptanceCriteria: Profile fetch query used during app init has `retry: 0` or `retry: 1` so failures resolve quickly.
A slow or unreachable backend during initialization does not keep the app on a loading screen for more than 5 seconds.
Query configuration changes do not break normal profile fetching when the backend is healthy.

constraints: Do not modify any file under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not change the backend schema or Motoko actor.
nonGoals: Redesigning the authentication flow beyond fixing the loading deadlock.
Changes to the LandingPage visual design or animations.
